# Scan GitLab for secrets

Connect GitLab as a data source to HCP Vault Radar to scan your GitLab
repositories for sensitive data and secrets. Vault Radar supports scanning GitLab
Cloud and GitLab Self-Managed instances.

@include 'vault-radar/radar-quickstart-tutorials.mdx'

## Prerequisites

- HCP IAM user with the HCP owner, or admin role
- [Vault Radar agent](/hcp/docs/vault-radar/agent/deploy) deployed and running (for agent scan)
- GitLab [personal access token with the required permission
  scopes](https://docs.gitlab.com/user/profile/personal_access_tokens/#create-a-personal-access-token):

  ```plaintext
  api
  read_api
  ```

## Add a GitLab data source

You can add and scan GitLab Cloud or GitLab Self-Managed instance using the
**HCP Vault Radar cloud scan** or **HCP Vault Radar agent scan**.

<Note>

Vault Radar has a limit of 5000 repositories per connection. If you have more
than 5000 repositories, Vault Radar will select the 5000 repositories with the
most recent activity.

</Note>

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="GitLab Cloud">

Add GitLab Cloud as a data source to scan your GitLab group's repositories for
secrets.

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="HCP Vault Radar cloud scan">

1. Log into the [HCP Portal](https://portal.cloud.hashicorp.com/sign-in) with an
   HCP IAM user that has the HCP owner or admin role.

1. Click **Vault Radar**.

1. Click **Settings**.

1. Click **Data Source Hosts**.

1. Click **Add data source host**.

1. Select **HCP Vault Radar Scan**.

1. Select **GitLab Cloud**.

1. Enter the personal access token in the **Enter your GitLab personal access
    token** field.

1. Click **Next**.

1. Select either **All data sources** or **Select data sources**.

1. Click **Finish**.

</Tab>
<Tab heading="HCP Vault Radar agent scan">

1. Log into the [HCP Portal](https://portal.cloud.hashicorp.com/sign-in) with an
   HCP IAM user that has the HCP owner or admin role.

1. Click **Vault Radar**.

1. Click **Settings**.

1. Click **Data Source Hosts**.

1. Click **Add data source host**.

1. Select **Vault Radar Agent Scan**.

1. Select **GitLab Cloud**.

1. Enter the path to the environment variable with the personal access token in
    the **Enter environment path to your GitLab personal access token** field.

    **Example:**

    <CodeBlockConfig hideClipboard>

    ```plaintext
    env://GITLAB_TOKEN
    ```

    </CodeBlockConfig>

1. Click **Next**.

1. Select either **All data sources** or **Select data sources**.

1. Click **Finish**.

</Tab>
</Tabs>

</Tab>
<Tab heading="GitLab Self-Managed instance">

Add GitLab Self-Managed instance as a data source to scan your GitLab group's
repositories for secrets.

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="HCP Vault Radar cloud scan">

1. Log into the [HCP Portal](https://portal.cloud.hashicorp.com/sign-in) with an
   HCP IAM user that has the HCP owner or admin role.

1. Click **Vault Radar**.

1. Click **Settings**.

1. Click **Data Source Hosts**.

1. Click **Add data source host**.

1. Select **HCP Vault Radar Scan**.

1. Select **GitLab Self-Managed instance**.

1. Enter the following information:

    - **Enter your GitLab domain name:** Fully qualified domain name of your
      GitLab Self-Managed instance.
    - **Enter your GitLab personal access token:** Access token from your GitLab
      Self-Managed instance.

1. Click **Next**.

1. Select either **All data sources** or **Select data sources**.

1. Click **Finish**.

</Tab>
<Tab heading="HCP Vault Radar agent scan">

1. Log into the [HCP Portal](https://portal.cloud.hashicorp.com/sign-in) with an
   HCP IAM user that has the HCP owner or admin role.

1. Click **Vault Radar**.

1. Click **Settings**.

1. Click **Data Source Hosts**.

1. Click **Add data source host**.

1. Select **Vault Radar Agent Scan**.

1. Select **GitLab Self-Managed instance**.

1. Enter the following information:

    - **Enter your GitLab domain name:** Fully qualified domain name of your
      GitLab Self-Managed instance.
    - **Enter environment path to your GitLab personal access token:** Access
      token from your GitLab Self-Managed instance.

    **Example:**

    <CodeBlockConfig hideClipboard>

    ```plaintext
    env://GITLAB_TOKEN
    ```

    </CodeBlockConfig>

1. Click **Next**.

1. Select either **All data sources** or **Select data sources**.

1. Click **Finish**.

</Tab>
</Tabs>

</Tab>
</Tabs>
